name: Namespace Created Followed by Multiple Resources Created on a Container Environment
notes:
This rule triggers when an unauthorized user creates a new namespace, followed by multiple resources creation in that namespace. Creating a namespace is a valid action for any user, but creating multiple resources in the namespace right after creating the namespace is suspicious.

Note: Edit the rule to replace "authorized_users" by typical administrators of the system.
bb_references: BB:CategoryDefinition: Resource Creation Events match at least 10 times with the same Namespace (custom) in 15 minutes after BB:BehaviourDefinition: Unauthorized User Creating Namespaces match
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Containers
when the event matches Username is not any of authorized_users
when BB:CategoryDefinition: Resource Creation Events match at least 10 times with the same Namespace (custom) in 15 minutes after BB:BehaviourDefinition: Unauthorized User Creating Namespaces match
thresholds: when BB:CategoryDefinition: Resource Creation Events match at least 10 times with the same Namespace (custom) in 15 minutes after BB:BehaviourDefinition: Unauthorized User Creating Namespaces match
TD_list: APPLY Namespace Created Followed by Multiple Resources Created on a Container Environment on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Containers
AND when the event matches Username is not any of authorized_users

AND when BB:CategoryDefinition: Resource Creation Events match at least 10 times with the same Namespace (custom) in 15 minutes after BB:BehaviourDefinition: Unauthorized User Creating Namespaces match
RED_list: Event name: Namespace Created Followed by Multiple Resources Created on a Container Environment
Event description: Detects new namespace created by unauthorized user, followed by multiple resources created.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Username
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
